sets.passage: Over the past decade, the gig economy has reshaped how millions of people earn a living. Instead of holding a single permanent job, many workers now take on short-term tasks through digital platforms that (1)____ them with customers in minutes. Drivers, delivery couriers, and freelance designers all (2)____ the freedom this model offers. They can decide when to work and how many hours to (3)____ each week. For some, this arrangement is a (4)____ blessing, because it allows them to balance income with family duties or study. The (5)____ appeal of such flexibility has drawn a growing crowd of young people who dislike rigid office routines. However, critics warn that the benefits are not (6)____ shared. Gig workers usually receive no paid holidays, no medical insurance, and little (7)____ against sudden loss of income. Their earnings can (8)____ sharply from one month to the next, making it hard to plan ahead. Governments in several countries have begun to (9)____ new rules that would grant these workers basic protections without destroying the flexibility they value. Companies, meanwhile, argue that heavy regulation could raise costs and reduce the number of available tasks. The debate is far from settled. What seems (10)____, though, is that the gig economy will remain a major feature of modern labour markets. Whether it ultimately empowers ordinary individuals or merely shifts financial risk onto them depends on how wisely society chooses to manage this powerful new force in the years that lie immediately ahead of all of us, and on whether workers themselves can organise to demand a fairer and more secure deal from the platforms they serve.
sets.explain_zh: (1)connect：platforms that connect them with customers，connect…with 搭配，主语关系从句需动词原形。(2)appreciate：appreciate the freedom 欣赏/珍视自由，及物动词接 freedom。(3)devote：how many hours to devote each week，devote 时间 投入，不定式动词原形。(4)mixed：a mixed blessing 固定短语“喜忧参半之事”，形容词修饰 blessing。(5)obvious：the obvious appeal 明显的吸引力，形容词修饰 appeal。(6)evenly：not evenly shared 平均地分享，副词修饰过去分词 shared。(7)protection：little protection against 抵御…的保护，名词，作宾语。(8)fluctuate：earnings can fluctuate sharply 收入波动，情态动词后接动词原形。(9)introduce：introduce new rules 推出新规则，动词原形。(10)certain：What seems certain 确定的，系动词 seems 后接形容词。干扰项：ignore 忽视、permanent 永久的(与flexibility矛盾)、loose 松散的、casual 随意的、afford 负担得起——语义或词性均无任何空格可填。

sets.passage: Starting a company from nothing is one of the boldest decisions a person can make. Every successful startup begins with an idea, but an idea alone is not enough. The founder must (1)____ that idea into a real product or service that people are willing to pay for. This process demands enormous (2)____, because the early years are full of setbacks and uncertainty. Investors will rarely hand over money unless they are (3)____ that the team can deliver. To win their trust, entrepreneurs prepare a detailed plan that explains how the business will (4)____ a profit and how quickly it can grow. Many young founders (5)____ the difficulty of attracting customers in a crowded market. They assume that a clever product will sell itself, but in reality marketing requires steady (6)____ and patience. A wise entrepreneur also surrounds herself with advisers whose experience can help her (7)____ costly mistakes. When the first sales finally arrive, the temptation is to expand too fast. (8)____ growth, however, can drain a company's cash and leave it (9)____ to sudden shocks. The healthiest startups grow at a pace they can control, reinvesting earnings instead of borrowing heavily. Above all, the founder must remain (10)____ in the face of repeated rejection, treating each failure as a lesson rather than a defeat. Those who combine vision with discipline, and courage with caution, stand the best chance of turning a fragile beginning into a lasting and genuinely rewarding enterprise that can endure for many decades to come.
sets.explain_zh: (1)transform：transform that idea into a product，transform…into 把…转化为，动词原形。(2)determination：demands enormous determination 巨大的决心，名词作宾语。(3)convinced：are convinced that 确信，be convinced 系表结构接 that 从句。(4)generate：generate a profit 创造利润，动词原形。(5)underestimate：underestimate the difficulty 低估难度，动词，搭配 difficulty。(6)effort：steady effort 持续的努力，名词。(7)avoid：help her avoid mistakes 避免错误，动词原形。(8)rapid：Rapid growth 快速增长，形容词修饰 growth。(9)vulnerable：leave it vulnerable to shocks 易受冲击，vulnerable to 搭配，作宾补形容词。(10)persistent：remain persistent 保持坚持，系动词 remain 后接形容词。干扰项：expensive 昂贵的、imagine 想象、delay 推迟、private 私人的、approve 批准——词性或语义均不与任一空匹配。

sets.passage: No matter how talented an individual may be, few great achievements in the modern workplace are reached alone. Effective teamwork allows a group to (1)____ goals that would be impossible for any single member. The first ingredient of a strong team is clear communication. When colleagues share information freely, misunderstandings are far less (2)____ to arise. A good leader also makes sure that every voice is heard, so that quieter members feel free to (3)____ their ideas. Diversity of background and skill is another (4)____ source of strength. People who think differently can spot problems and solutions that a uniform group might (5)____ entirely. Of course, differences can also create friction, and disagreements must be handled with (6)____ rather than anger. Teams that learn to argue about ideas without attacking one another tend to produce the most (7)____ results. Trust lies at the very heart of the matter. Members must believe that their colleagues will (8)____ their promises and carry their fair share of the load. When trust breaks down, even the most skilled group will gradually (9)____ apart. Finally, shared recognition keeps motivation high; celebrating success together reminds everyone that the credit (10)____ to the whole team, not to one star performer. Companies that invest in building these habits discover that the combined energy of a united group can far exceed the simple sum of its parts, driving both productivity and lasting satisfaction across the entire organisation over the long run for many years. Such teams, built patiently and led with genuine care, become the quiet engines that allow ambitious companies to outperform far larger and wealthier competitors again and again.
sets.explain_zh: (1)accomplish：accomplish goals 完成目标，动词原形接 goals。(2)likely：less likely to arise 不太可能发生，be likely to 结构，形容词。(3)contribute：contribute their ideas 贡献想法，动词原形。(4)valuable：valuable source of strength 宝贵的源泉，形容词修饰 source。(5)overlook：might overlook entirely 完全忽视，情态动词后动词原形，语义“看漏”。(6)patience：with patience 以耐心，介词后名词，与 anger 对比。(7)creative：creative results 有创造力的结果，形容词修饰 results。(8)honour：honour their promises 信守承诺，固定搭配，动词原形。(9)fall：fall apart 散架/瓦解，固定短语 fall apart。(10)belongs：the credit belongs to the whole team 功劳属于，belong to 搭配，主语 credit 第三人称单数。干扰项：complete 完成/完整、ceiling 天花板(具体名词，任何空均无法填入)、refuse 拒绝、demand 要求、rise 上升——语义或搭配均不能唯一填入任何空。

sets.passage: Modern advertising encourages us to believe that happiness can be bought. From glowing screens, a constant stream of images (1)____ us to desire products we did not even know existed. This pressure has helped create a culture in which many people spend far beyond their means. The (2)____ to own the newest phone or the trendiest clothes can be surprisingly difficult to resist. Credit cards make the problem worse, because they allow shoppers to (3)____ purchases without feeling the immediate pain of payment. As a result, household debt has risen to (4)____ levels in many wealthy nations. Psychologists point out that the pleasure of a new purchase tends to (5)____ quickly, leaving the buyer eager for the next thrill. This endless cycle rarely brings (6)____ satisfaction. Instead, it often produces stress, regret, and a nagging sense that something is missing. Some shoppers admit that they buy things simply to (7)____ boredom or sadness, a habit that empties the wallet without healing the mind. Thoughtful consumers are beginning to (8)____ this trend. They ask themselves whether a purchase reflects a genuine need or is merely a sudden (9)____ they acted on without thinking. By pausing before they buy, they regain control over their finances and their attention. Living with less, they discover, can feel surprisingly (10)____, freeing both money and time for the relationships and experiences that truly matter. In a world carefully built to make us want more and more, choosing to be content with enough may turn out to be one of the wisest and most quietly rewarding decisions a thoughtful person can ever make for themselves and their family.
sets.explain_zh: (1)persuades：images persuade(s) us to desire，persuade sb to do，主语 stream 第三人称单数。(2)temptation：The temptation to own…difficult to resist 拥有的诱惑，名词接不定式；resist temptation 为固定搭配，此处唯一锁定 temptation。(3)delay：delay purchases 推迟付款，动词原形，语义信用卡让人延后付款痛苦。(4)alarming：alarming levels 惊人的水平，形容词修饰 levels。(5)fade：pleasure tends to fade 快感消退，不定式动词原形。(6)lasting：lasting satisfaction 持久的满足，形容词修饰 satisfaction。(7)escape：escape boredom 逃避无聊，动词原形。(8)resist：resist this trend 抵制趋势，动词原形。(9)impulse：a sudden impulse they acted on 一时冲动，名词；act on an impulse 为固定搭配（不可 act on a temptation），与 need 对比，此处唯一锁定 impulse。(10)liberating：feel liberating 感到解放，形容词作表语。干扰项：convince(需 convince sb，无法填(1)因主语单数且语境persuade更准——实为 persuades 唯一；convince 不接to do同结构)、frequent 频繁的、remind(remind sb of/to)、shortage 短缺、purchase 购买——均无空可唯一填入。

sets.passage: Saving money is a habit that rewards those who practise it early and consistently. Many young people assume there is plenty of time to start later, yet this delay can (1)____ them dearly. The magic of compound interest means that even small amounts, set aside (2)____, can grow into a substantial sum over the years. The first step is to build an emergency fund, a cushion of cash that can cover unexpected expenses such as a medical bill or a sudden (3)____ of income. Without this buffer, a single misfortune may force a family into debt. Financial advisers usually recommend that people (4)____ a fixed share of every paycheck before they spend a penny on anything else. This simple rule prevents the (5)____ to splurge from swallowing the whole salary. It also helps to (6)____ clear goals, such as a deposit for a home or a comfortable retirement, because a concrete target makes sacrifice feel (7)____. Of course, saving demands discipline, and discipline can be (8)____ to maintain when friends are enjoying expensive holidays. Yet those who (9)____ short-term pleasures for long-term security often find that peace of mind is worth the price. A modest lifestyle today can purchase genuine freedom tomorrow. In the end, wealth is built less by a high income than by the steady, (10)____ choice to live below one's means and let patience do the rest, year after quiet year for many decades. The person who masters this humble discipline in youth rarely worries about money in old age, while the spendthrift who postpones it often labours anxiously long after others have comfortably retired.
sets.explain_zh: (1)cost：can cost them dearly 让他们付出代价，cost sb 结构，动词原形。(2)regularly：set aside regularly 定期存钱，副词。(3)loss：a sudden loss of income 收入骤失，名词，loss of。(4)set：set a fixed share 留出固定份额，动词原形，set aside之意。(5)temptation：the temptation to splurge 挥霍的诱惑，名词接不定式。(6)establish：establish clear goals 设立明确目标，动词原形。(7)worthwhile：makes sacrifice feel worthwhile 值得的，形容词作宾补。(8)hard：can be hard to maintain 难以维持，系动词后形容词。(9)sacrifice：sacrifice short-term pleasures 牺牲短期快乐，动词原形。(10)deliberate：the deliberate choice 深思熟虑的选择，形容词修饰 choice。干扰项：spend 花费(与saving矛盾)、rarely 很少、gain 获得、envelope 信封(具体名词，任何空均无法填入)、borrow 借——语义或词性均无唯一空可填。

sets.passage: Choosing a career is among the most important decisions a young person ever faces, yet many drift into a job by chance rather than design. A thoughtful approach begins with honest self-examination. Before chasing any title, you should (1)____ your own strengths, interests, and values, asking what kind of work would truly (2)____ you year after year. Money matters, of course, but a salary alone seldom provides lasting (3)____. People who ignore their passions for the sake of a fat paycheck often grow restless and (4)____ within a few years. It also pays to research the job market carefully, because some fields are shrinking while others are expanding (5)____. A wise candidate studies which skills employers will (6)____ in the coming decade and works to acquire them in advance. Mentors can offer (7)____ advice that no textbook contains, sharing the hard lessons of real experience. Be prepared, however, for the path to twist unexpectedly; few careers today follow a straight line. The ability to (8)____ to change has become as valuable as any single qualification. Above all, do not let the fear of making a mistake (9)____ you completely, for an imperfect choice followed by genuine effort usually beats endless hesitation. A career is not a single decision but a long series of them, and the (10)____ to keep learning will serve you far better than any plan fixed rigidly in your youth and never once revised again. Treat your working life as an unfolding journey rather than a fixed destination, and you will discover opportunities that no anxious teenager could possibly have foreseen at the very start.
sets.explain_zh: (1)assess：assess your strengths 评估优势，动词原形。(2)fulfil：work would fulfil you 让你有成就感，动词原形。(3)satisfaction：lasting satisfaction 持久的满足，名词。(4)dissatisfied：grow restless and dissatisfied 不满的，形容词，与 restless 并列。(5)rapidly：expanding rapidly 快速扩张，副词。(6)demand：employers will demand 雇主将需要(技能)，动词原形，宾语 skills。(7)valuable：valuable advice 宝贵建议，形容词修饰 advice。(8)adapt：adapt to change 适应变化，adapt to 搭配，动词原形。(9)paralyse：fear paralyse you 让你瘫痪/动弹不得，动词原形，宾语 you。(10)willingness：the willingness to keep learning 持续学习的意愿，名词接不定式。干扰项：ignore 忽视、frequent 频繁的、supply 供应、useless 无用的、decide 决定——语义或搭配均无法唯一填入任何空。

sets.passage: In today's fiercely competitive markets, the companies that thrive are usually those that never stop searching for better ways of doing things. Innovation in the workplace, however, does not happen by accident; it must be deliberately (1)____. Leaders who want fresh ideas first need to create a culture in which employees feel safe to (2)____ unconventional suggestions without fear of ridicule. When a worker proposes something risky and it fails, a wise manager treats the failure as a (3)____ experiment rather than a punishable error. This attitude (4)____ people to keep experimenting. Equally important is giving staff the time and resources to explore. Some of the world's most famous products were born when engineers were (5)____ to spend a portion of each week on projects of their own choosing. Cross-department collaboration also sparks creativity, because a problem that (6)____ one team may yield instantly to the fresh perspective of another. Yet innovation is not merely about generating ideas; it is about turning the best of them into reality. A brilliant concept is (7)____ unless someone has the determination to develop it, test it, and bring it to customers. Managers must therefore (8)____ a steady flow of small improvements alongside the occasional bold breakthrough. They should reward not only the rare genius but also the (9)____ contributor who refines a process step by step. Firms that weave these practices into their daily routine tend to remain (10)____, adapting smoothly to change long after their slower and more cautious rivals have quietly faded from the scene forever.
sets.explain_zh: (1)cultivated：must be deliberately cultivated 被有意培养，被动语态过去分词。(2)voice：safe to voice unconventional suggestions 说出建议，voice 作动词原形。(3)valuable：a valuable experiment 有价值的实验，形容词修饰 experiment，与 punishable error 对比。(4)encourages：This attitude encourages people 鼓励，主语 attitude 第三人称单数。(5)permitted：were permitted to spend 被允许，被动语态过去分词接不定式。(6)puzzles：a problem that puzzles one team 难住一队，主语 problem 第三人称单数。(7)worthless：is worthless unless 毫无价值，形容词作表语。(8)sustain：sustain a steady flow 维持稳定的流，动词原形。(9)modest：the modest contributor 谦逊/不起眼的贡献者，形容词修饰 contributor，与 rare genius 对比。(10)competitive：remain competitive 保持竞争力，系动词后形容词。干扰项：ignore 忽视、obvious 明显的、forbidden 被禁止的(与语境矛盾)、frequent 频繁的、destroy 破坏——均无唯一空可填。

sets.passage: Globalization has knitted the economies of the world together more tightly than at any point in history. Goods produced in one country are now shipped across oceans to (1)____ the demands of consumers thousands of miles away. A single smartphone may contain parts (2)____ from a dozen different nations before it reaches the buyer. This vast web of exchange brings clear (3)____. Trade allows each country to specialise in what it does best, which tends to lower prices and (4)____ the variety of products on the shelves. Developing nations, in particular, have used exports to (5)____ millions of people out of poverty. Yet the picture is not entirely bright. When factories move abroad in search of cheaper labour, workers in wealthier countries may suddenly (6)____ their jobs. Local industries that cannot (7)____ with foreign rivals sometimes collapse altogether, leaving whole towns struggling. Critics also warn that long supply chains are (8)____ to disruption, as recent shortages have painfully shown. A storm, a strike, or a political quarrel on the far side of the planet can (9)____ shelves empty within days. For these reasons, governments are now rethinking how much they should depend on distant suppliers. The challenge is to keep the (10)____ benefits of open trade while reducing its dangers. Few experts believe globalization will reverse, but most expect it to grow more cautious in the years ahead, carefully balancing the efficiency of open markets against the security of having key goods produced closer to home, where supply can be more reliably guaranteed even when distant troubles strike.
sets.explain_zh: (1)satisfy：satisfy the demands 满足需求，动词原形。(2)sourced：parts sourced from a dozen nations 来源于，过去分词作后置定语。(3)benefits：brings clear benefits 带来明显好处，复数名词作宾语。(4)expand：expand the variety 扩大种类，动词原形，与 lower prices 并列。(5)lift：lift millions out of poverty 使脱贫，lift…out of 搭配，动词原形。(6)lose：lose their jobs 失业，动词原形。(7)compete：compete with foreign rivals 与对手竞争，compete with 搭配，动词原形。(8)vulnerable：are vulnerable to disruption 易受冲击，be vulnerable to，形容词。(9)leave：leave shelves empty 使货架空空，leave + 宾语 + 形容词，动词原形。(10)genuine：the genuine benefits 真正的好处，形容词修饰 benefits。干扰项：reduce 减少、telescope 望远镜(具体名词，任何空均无法填入)、advantage 优势(名词单数，无法填复数(3))、secure 安全的/获得、fill 填满——语义或词性均无唯一空匹配。

sets.passage: For generations, hard work was treated as the supreme virtue, and long hours at the office were worn as a badge of honour. Today, however, a growing number of people are questioning whether this devotion to the job is worth the (1)____ it imposes on health and family. The pursuit of a healthy work-life balance has become a central concern. When employees are (2)____ to answer emails late at night and through the weekend, the boundary between work and rest gradually disappears. Such constant pressure can (3)____ both mind and body, leading to exhaustion that psychologists now call burnout. Research consistently shows that overworked staff are actually less (4)____ than colleagues who rest properly, because tired brains make more mistakes. Wise employers have therefore begun to (5)____ flexible schedules and generous leave, recognising that a rested worker is a (6)____ one. Setting clear limits is the responsibility of the individual as well. Learning to (7)____ extra tasks politely, and to switch off devices after hours, protects the precious time that belongs to oneself and one's loved ones. A satisfying life cannot be measured by a paycheck alone; it also (8)____ on friendships, hobbies, and quiet moments of rest. Those who guard these things tend to feel more (9)____ overall, and ironically they often perform better at work too. The lesson, slowly being learned across many industries, is that treating people as whole human beings rather than mere instruments of production ultimately (10)____ everyone, employer and employee alike, in lasting ways.
sets.explain_zh: (1)price：the price it imposes 它带来的代价，名词，impose a price on。(2)expected：are expected to answer emails 被期望，被动语态过去分词接不定式。(3)harm：can harm both mind and body 损害身心，动词原形。(4)productive：less productive 效率较低，形容词作表语。(5)offer：offer flexible schedules 提供弹性工作制，动词原形。(6)productive 已用——此处(6)a … one 指 worker，需 valuable：a valuable one 有价值的(员工)，形容词。(7)decline：decline extra tasks politely 婉拒额外任务，动词原形。(8)depends：it also depends on 取决于，depend on 搭配，主语 it 第三人称单数。(9)content：feel more content 更满足，形容词作表语。(10)benefits：ultimately benefits everyone 最终惠及每个人，主语第三人称单数，动词。干扰项：garden 花园(具体名词，任何空均无法填入)、wooden 木制的(形容词，语义荒谬，任何空均无法填入)、improve 改善、circular 圆形的(形容词，语义荒谬，任何空均无法填入)、rely(rely on 但(8)depends已唯一)——经语义与唯一性排查均无另一空可填。
